slots.woman: PEOPLE THINK I GOT RICH SELLING HOUSES
slots.cat: I am eating a gas station burrito in my car between showings

caption_draft.ig: Commission check: $12,400. After split, taxes, photos, staging, MLS fees, signage, and the $47 of open-house snacks nobody touched: I made less than the Uber driver who brought the buyer to the showing.

calibration_notes: Woman side = the public perception (all caps because that's the screaming accusation). Cat side = the deadpan reality. The gas station burrito is a devastatingly specific image every working agent has lived. Caption is the commission waterfall — math that any agent recognizes as their own paycheck. The Uber driver punchline is the unexpected gut punch.
